summary: DHH responds to criticism that Active Record in Rails tightly couples business objects to the ORM by openly acknowledging the tradeoff. He argues that marrying business objects to ActiveRecord::Base is a deliberate design choice, not an oversight. The benefit is a much more natural API compared to the Data Mapper pattern. He accepts that this means you can't swap persistence implementations, but considers that an acceptable cost.
key_points: Active Record intentionally couples business objects to the ORM in exchange for a more natural API
The inability to swap persistence implementations is an acknowledged and accepted tradeoff
DHH frames this as a pragmatic design choice rather than a limitation
money_quotes: I willingly pay the price of marrying my business objects to the ActiveRecord::Base to get a much more natural API in return.
No, I can't swap persistance implementations. That's the cost.
key_insight: Tight coupling between business objects and persistence is a worthwhile tradeoff when it yields a dramatically more natural and productive API.
